horizon_eth_manager.role: Ethereum-side bridge manager. Held the 4 operator hot-wallet addresses and ran the 2-of-4 ECDSA threshold check before unlocking the bucket. Modelled by HorizonEthManager.sol extending the shared MockMultisig harness (N=4, K=2).

horizon_eth_bucket.role: Custody pool that held the locked ETH/USDC/USDT/WBTC/DAI/etc. balances. Released funds only at the manager's request — modelled by EthBucket.sol with onlyManager guard.

weth_ethereum.role: WETH / native ETH leg — 13,100 ETH (~$14M) drained.

usdc_ethereum.role: USDC — ~$36M drained, the largest single asset.

dai_ethereum.role: DAI — drained alongside USDC/USDT/WBTC. Bundled in batched receipts.

attacker.role: Placeholder for the attacker EOA that received the 11 batched withdrawals. Real attribution: Lazarus Group (DPRK), per Elliptic forensic analysis 2022-06-29.

off_chain_compromise_note.reason: Same shape as Ronin (Sprint R): 2 of 4 operator hot-wallet keys leaked off-chain. The on-chain 2-of-4 ECDSA threshold check enforced its spec correctly — the failed assumption is 'fewer than 2 of the 4 hot wallets will be compromised at once'. Production hot-wallet management at Harmony was less well-documented than Ronin's Axie DAO delegate path, but the structural failure mode is identical.
off_chain_compromise_note.implication: Module 1 should infer the same `validator_set_authority` invariant as Ronin (modulo N/K). Module 2's RAG retrieval ought to surface the Ronin `key_compromise` scenario template here — Sprint R + Sprint H together stress whether the LLM generalises the multi-sig pattern across two structurally-similar incidents with different N/K.
off_chain_compromise_note.modelled_by: HorizonEthManager extends MockMultisig (N=4, K=2). Custody is delegated to a separate EthBucket so the (manager -> bucket) authority-delegation edge is explicit in the ATG.

root_cause_summary.vulnerable_pattern: Bridge withdrawal authorisation requires 2-of-4 ECDSA signatures from a fixed operator set. The on-chain check is correct.
root_cause_summary.verification_bypass: Off-chain: Harmony stored at least 2 of the 4 operator private keys on a hot-wallet infrastructure that fell to a malware-laden phishing campaign attributed to the Lazarus Group. Once 2 keys were on attacker-controlled hardware, the threshold was satisfied from a single attacker identity.
root_cause_summary.notes: The 2-of-4 threshold is the most aggressively-low ratio in the off-chain plan (50%); compromising even a single additional key would have collapsed the security argument. Compared to Ronin's 5-of-9 (55.6%) the smaller absolute count (2 vs 5) reduces the attacker's burden disproportionately.

exploit_characteristics.style: validator quorum collapse via private-key compromise, batched withdrawals
exploit_characteristics.notes: 11 batched withdrawal transactions executed across roughly 5 Ethereum blocks starting 15011935. The batching pattern is the per-asset distinction — each receipt corresponds to one (token, recipient, amount) tuple from the bucket.

references.title: Harmony — Lessons from the Harmony Bridge Hack
Elliptic — Harmony Horizon Bridge $100M Hack May Be Linked to Lazarus Group
